subterraCodes.title: Subterra Codes
subterraCodes.subtitle: Redeem working Subterra codes for Chrono Shards, TNT Blocks, EXP boosts, potions, and other progression rewards.
subterraCodes.intro: Use these codes at the Codes NPC in the surface lobby. Complete the tutorial first, then interact with the Codes NPC near the spawn gate, paste a code, and press Claim Code.

activeCodes.bestFor: Early Ability Card pulls and progression currency.
Mining runs, return safety, and ability-card progression.
Clearing terrain faster and leveling during focused play sessions.
Large Chrono Shard boost for cards and character growth.
Longer mining and combat sessions where EXP gain matters.
Starter progression after reaching the lobby.

redeemSteps: Complete the tutorial until you can freely move around the surface lobby.
Find the Codes NPC near the spawn gate.
Interact with the NPC, enter one working code, and press Claim Code.
Repeat the process for each active code.

subterraBeginnerGuide.title: Subterra Beginner Guide
subterraBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the basic loop: mine resources, manage inventory, upgrade your pickaxe and backpack, build with Ability Cards, craft gear, and survive deeper layers.
subterraBeginnerGuide.intro: Subterra is a 2.5D mining adventure where the world is procedurally generated and the layers become more hostile as you dig deeper. The clean starter route is simple: learn the controls, mine ores, upgrade tools, add Ability Cards, craft weapons or potions, then push into deeper layers while keeping each run profitable.

steps.title: Complete the Tutorial and Reach the Surface Lobby
steps.goal: Unlock free movement and learn where key NPCs and gates are located.
actions: Finish the opening tutorial.
Locate the spawn gate area.
Find the Codes NPC for reward redemption.
Use the lobby as your reset point before deeper mining runs.

steps.title: Learn the Core Controls
steps.goal: Move, mine, interact, block, and manage inventory without losing time underground.

steps.title: Mine Ores and Track Your Next Upgrade
steps.goal: Collect the materials needed for stronger pickaxes and larger backpacks.
actions: Use Left Mouse to mine blocks and ores.
Prioritize materials required by your next pickaxe or backpack tier.
Keep inventory space open for valuable ores.
Return to the surface when your bag is full or your run becomes risky.

steps.title: Upgrade Pickaxe First, Backpack Second
steps.goal: Improve mining power and speed before pushing too deep.
actions: Upgrade your pickaxe to mine faster and handle tougher layers.
Upgrade your backpack when short inventory runs start slowing progress.
Use early Gold, Rock, Coal, Copper, Tin, and Ingots for the first upgrade chain.

steps.title: Build Around Ability Cards
steps.goal: Use Ability Cards to shape your mining and survival style.
actions: Spend Chrono Shards on card progression.
Equip cards that improve your run efficiency.
Use code rewards to speed up early card access.

steps.title: Craft Weapons, Potions, and Recipes
steps.goal: Prepare for monsters, bosses, and longer underground runs.
actions: Craft weapons to fight enemies below the surface.
Look for rare weapon and potion recipes.
Use consumables before longer mining pushes.

steps.title: Explore Layers and Structures
steps.goal: Find deeper resources, lootable chests, cave structures, and progression materials.
actions: Move layer by layer instead of rushing straight down.
Open chests and inspect structures when safe.
Watch for stronger monsters as the depth increases.

steps.title: Fight Carefully and Use Block or Parry
steps.goal: Survive hostile layers while keeping mining runs profitable.
actions: Use F to block or parry incoming attacks.
Keep weapons ready when entering hostile sections.
Retreat when enemies or bosses make the run too expensive.

subterraPickaxeAndBackpackUpgrades.title: Subterra Pickaxe and Backpack Upgrades
subterraPickaxeAndBackpackUpgrades.subtitle: Plan early Subterra progression with pickaxe power, mining speed, backpack space, and upgrade recipes.
subterraPickaxeAndBackpackUpgrades.intro: Pickaxe upgrades raise your mining power and speed, while backpack upgrades increase inventory space. The first stretch is where materials are easiest to misallocate, so use the tier data below before spending.

pickaxeRows.priorityNote: Use this only long enough to gather Rock and Gold for Tier 2.
First practical upgrade for smoother surface mining.
Adds Coal to the early material route.
Start banking Raw Copper and Coal instead of spending them randomly.
Moves the route into Copper Ingot crafting.
A strong early jump in power; begin saving Tin materials.
Good checkpoint before heavier Tin and Copper costs.
Coal and Copper Ingot demand increases; avoid dumping them into side spending.
Citrine enters the route; keep gem drops for upgrades.
Begin collecting Raw Iron while maintaining Coal supply.
Material variety increases; track each ingredient before crafting side items.
Iron Ingot becomes central to the route.
Clean early-mid checkpoint before Silver-tier recipes start appearing.

backpackRows.priorityNote: Use until mining trips start ending because of inventory space.
First inventory upgrade for longer beginner mining loops.
Pairs well with early Copper and Tin progression.
Upgrade when Iron materials become common enough.
A midgame storage step that uses Silver and gem materials.
Save Amethyst and Emerald instead of burning them too early.
High-value gem materials start becoming important.
Platinum and Sapphire enter the storage path.
Diamond appears; do not spend rare gems blindly.
Large final crafted backpack checkpoint before gamepass storage.

priorities.title: Upgrade Pickaxe When Mining Feels Slow
priorities.description: Pickaxe tiers increase both power and mining speed, so they unlock faster routes through tougher blocks and deeper layers.

priorities.title: Upgrade Backpack When Trips End Too Early
priorities.description: Backpack upgrades are best when your inventory fills before your mining route is finished.

priorities.title: Save Gems for Required Recipes
priorities.description: Citrine, Topaz, Amethyst, Emerald, Ruby, Sapphire, and Diamond appear in upgrade recipes, so spending them early can delay later tiers.

priorities.title: Use Codes to Accelerate Card and EXP Progress
priorities.description: Chrono Shards, TNT Blocks, EXP boosts, and potions support the same mining loop that feeds pickaxe and backpack progression.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Subterra

questions.question: What is Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra is a 2.5D Roblox mining adventure by Polyworks Studio where players dig through hostile layers, upgrade pickaxes and backpacks, build with Ability Cards, craft weapons, and fight monsters underground.

questions.question: Where can I play Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra is available on Roblox. You can launch it directly from the official Roblox game page on PC, mobile, and supported consoles.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Subterra?
questions.answer: In Subterra, complete the tutorial, then interact with the Codes NPC near the spawn gate in the surface lobby, enter a working code, and press Claim Code to receive your rewards.

questions.question: What are Ability Cards in Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra Ability Cards are equippable modifiers bought with Chrono Shards. They improve mining, backpack space, movement, smelting, and combat, letting you shape your own mining and survival style.

questions.question: How do layers work in Subterra?
questions.answer: Subterra layers are procedurally generated cave levels that become more hostile as you dig deeper. Each layer changes the block pool, ore value, structures, and enemies, so upgrade your pickaxe and gear before pushing down.

questions.question: Is Subterra free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Subterra is free to play on Roblox, with optional gamepass storage and cosmetic upgrades that do not block core mining progression.
